growth_picks.name: Newmont Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 45.8% — well above 30% threshold, driven by gold price strength and post-Newcrest synergies.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 67.4%, exceeds 65% threshold despite mining cost structure — operating margin also exceptional at 61.4%.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 45.8 + 61.4 = 107.2, more than 2x the 50 threshold.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 78.6% > revenue growth 45.8% — clear operating leverage from rising gold prices on a roughly fixed cost base.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 25.83% above 20% threshold — strong for a capital-intensive miner.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.03% (well under 5%) and declining 10.55% MoM — no bearish positioning.

ai_exposure.detail: Gold mining business is not directly exposed to AI demand or competitive threat. Some indirect operational efficiency benefit possible but immaterial to thesis.

deal_radar.assessment: Forward P/E 10.15 with 45.8% revenue growth and 78.6% earnings growth is the screen highlight; defensive beta 0.448 makes this a hedge that also has its own catalyst from gold strength.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Newmont scores a clean 6/6 with exceptional operating leverage (EG 78.6% on RG 45.8%), 67% gross margins, and 25% ROE while still trading at 10x forward earnings — a rare combination. The 25% gap to consensus mean target plus declining short interest and a defensive 0.45 beta makes this a high-conviction long with built-in regime hedging. Primary risk is gold price reversal, but the cost-curve operating leverage gives downside cushion.

Beta 2.37 means this is a high-volatility position requiring sizing discipline, but the AI tailwind and 36% gap-to-target make it the best growth-at-a-reasonable-price name in the set.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Reverts to 20-day SMA on momentum-rotation; fundamentals intact.
Reverts to 50-day SMA on broader risk-off or rate-cut acceleration that compresses net interest income.
Hits analyst low target — would require Fed-cut path materially compressing margin lending and account NII spread.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Between 20-day ($78.59) and 50-day ($72.41) SMAs after a momentum reset; provides ~15% upside to median target rather than chasing into the 52-week high.

comparables.note: Charles Schwab — retail-broker peer; lower forward multiple, higher cash-sorting risk — not in input.
Robinhood — retail-flow peer; faster growth, lower margin profile, more crypto-flow sensitivity — not in input.

growth_picks.analyst_take: IBKR is a clean fundamental story (5.5/6, ROE 23.6%, declining shorts) but tactically extended — at 52-week highs, 21% above the 20-day SMA, with mean analyst target below current price. The setup favors patience: watchlist into a $75 mean-reversion entry rather than buying at consensus. If estimates step up post-print, the framework still passes; valuation discipline matters more than thesis here.
